response: The world's greatest Super-Heroes anthology showcased one of four superheroes known for speaking the phrase "SHAZAM," which was Captain Marvel. Captain Marvel, also known as Shazam, is a fictional superhero appearing in American comic books published by DC Comics. The character was created by artist C. C. Beck and writer Bill Parker in 1939 and first appeared in "Whiz Comics" #2 (cover-dated Feb. 1940). Captain Marvel is the alter ego of Billy Batson, a boy who, by speaking the magic word "SHAZAM," can transform into a costumed adult with superhuman powers.
